title: AI-Naama
author: Wadhwani AI
description:
Wadhwani AI was founded on the conviction that technology must serve the underserved. AI-Naama — meaning "a register of AI" or "an account of AI" in Urdu and Hindi — extends that mission into the public sphere. It is a podcast series by Wadhwani AI that brings rigorous, accessible discourse about AI and subsequent digital public goods to a broad public audience.


AI systems are increasingly shaping consequential decisions in health, agriculture, education, credit, and governance – potentially in ways that could be opaque, inequitable, or unaccountable. These risks are especially acute in the Global South, where regulatory frameworks are nascent, affected communities are underrepresented in the design of these systems, and the stakes of getting AI wrong are highest.


AI-Naama convenes government, researchers, practitioners, policymakers, and civil society voices to examine what responsible, ethical AI actually looks like in practice - not as an abstract ideal, but as a contested, urgent, and evolving project.

title: In This Moment with Toby C.
author: Mutual Broadcasting
description:
Helping Open Hearts and Minds in a World of Overstimulation Life moves fast. Stress, anxiety, addiction, unhealthy relationships, fear, and loneliness can leave us feeling overwhelmed and disconnected. In This Moment with Toby C. invites listeners to slow down, take a breath, and discover that lasting recovery and personal transformation begin by becoming fully present. Hosted by longtime recovery advocate Toby C., the program draws upon the enduring wisdom of the Twelve Steps while presenting universal principles that apply to anyone seeking a healthier, more meaningful life. Although addiction recovery is often the starting point for the conversation, the message reaches far beyond alcohol and drugs. Every episode speaks to the human experience—our need for connection, purpose, faith, humility, forgiveness, and hope. Listeners are encouraged to move beyond simply surviving and begin truly living through practical discussions about: 
•	Recovery and emotional healing 
•	Overcoming fear, shame, and isolation 
•	Healthy relationships and personal growth 
•	Spiritual principles for everyday living 
•	Sponsorship, service, and helping others 
•	Finding purpose through faith and community 
•	Living one day—and one moment—at a time 
The program isn't about perfection. It's about progress. It's about discovering that every setback can become a stepping stone toward a better future. Whether you're beginning your own recovery journey, supporting a loved one, or simply searching for greater peace and direction, In This Moment with Toby C. offers a message of encouragement that resonates with listeners everywhere. Recovery is possible. Hope is real. Your next chapter can begin right now... in this moment.

Have you ever stood in line and wondered whether you were trapped inside a hidden-camera prank show? Have you dealt with a worker, customer, company or everyday situation that made absolutely no logical sense?
You are not alone.
Each week, Mr. Semilla explores the inefficient, ridiculous and hilarious situations people face in stores, workplaces, relationships and daily life. He breaks down the drama, finds the humor and shares useful tools, products and strategies that can help you handle life when common sense disappears.

Every episode is one lighthouse. Every lighthouse has a story.
Some of them are about the people who kept the lights burning — alone, for decades, through storms that took everything else. The woman who held the lamp through a month-long gale while the sea swept her house away. The keeper who rowed into the harbor, again and again, because that was the job. The man who climbed eighty-seven steps every day for thirty years after anyone stopped paying him to.
Some of them are about the structures themselves — what it actually took to build something that could stand where the ocean was trying, specifically, to kill it. What happened on the days it lost.
Each episode runs about two minutes. One lighthouse, one story, one reason it earned its place.
See the light.

Welcome to Does My Pet Even Like Me?, the podcast for the beautifully anxious, slightly neurotic, and deeply devoted modern pet parent. If you’ve ever stayed up until 2:00 AM researching if your dog’s food bowl is triggering their sensory issues, or felt personally offended by the way your cat looked at you from across the room , congratulations, you have found your support group.

Each week, we are diving deep into the everyday anxieties of pet ownership. We aren't just talking about basic training; we are tackling the real stuff:

  • The Overthinking: From choosing the right food to understanding why they hate grooming.
  • The Science: Speaking to veterinary behaviorists to decode pet anxiety and weird behaviors.
  • The Psychology: Sitting down with human psychologists to explore how pets heal our mental health, help people with autism, and why an entire generation is choosing "fur babies" over actual babies.

Whether you're trying to break the cycle of household stress, looking for expert advice from people in the field, or just want to feel less alone in your pet-parenting panic, this show is for you.

Every episode follows the Startup Sequence, a six-stage arc from the life they left to the one they built — including Max Q, which is the moment the whole thing nearly came apart. description:

Through it all, I've learned that life is about giving back, paying the good forward and helping others live more fulfilling and rewarding lives. 


On Training, Tumours & Trauma, we have deep, honest and sometimes uncomfortable conversations about training, mindset, nutrition, music, movies, hobbies, gaming, resilience, recovery and the challenges that shape us. title: 電子タバコは何歳から吸える？法律とマナー、健康リスクを徹底解説
author: lin lin
description:
「成人年齢が18歳に引き下げられたけれど、電子タバコは何歳からOKなの？」という疑問を抱く方は少なくありません。特に最近は、スタイリッシュなデザインや多彩なフレーバーの登場により、若い世代の間でもVAPE（ベイプ）への関心が高まっています。今回は、2026年現在の最新状況に基づき、電子タバコの利用年齢や法的な位置付けについて詳しく解説します。
まず、基本的な定義として、 電子タバコとは？ ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/vape-beginner-guide/ ) という点を確認しておきましょう。これはタバコ葉を燃焼させるのではなく、食品添加物などにも使われる成分と香料を混ぜた「リキッド」を電気で加熱し、その蒸気を吸い込むデバイスを指します。一方、アイコス（IQOS）などの「加熱式タバコ」はタバコ葉を使用するため、法律上は20歳未満の喫煙が厳格に禁止されています。
では、ニコチンを含まない電子タバコ（VAPE）はどうでしょうか。法的な解釈では、ニコチンなしのリキッドはタバコ製品に分類されないため、 電子タバコ 未成年者喫煙禁止法 ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/vape-underage-law-risk/ ) （現在の二十歳未満ノ喫煙ノ禁止ニ関スル法律）の直接的な対象外となる場合があります。しかし、ここが重要なポイントですが、日本国内の主要メーカーや販売店、コンビニ各社では、青少年の健康保護と喫煙習慣の誘発防止を目的として、独自の「自主規制」を敷いています。そのため、実店舗やオンラインショップの多くでは、購入時に20歳以上であることを確認する年齢認証が必須となっています。
利用を検討する際に避けて通れないのが、「 電子タバコ 害はあるのか ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/vape-health-risks/ ) 」という問題です。ニコチンやタールが含まれていないとはいえ、蒸気を直接肺に吸い込む行為が身体に与える長期的な影響については、現在も世界中で研究が進められている段階です。特に成長期にある若年層にとっては、呼吸器への刺激や未知の成分による健康リスクが懸念されるため、安易な使用は推奨されません。
こうした複雑な年齢制限の背景や、正しいデバイスの選び方、そして2026年の最新トレンド情報を網羅的に発信しているのが、 KYOTO VAPE 電子タバコ 最新情報ガイド ( https://blog.kyotovape.com/ ) です。
結論として、電子タバコは「法的なグレーゾーンはあるものの、20歳になってから楽しむもの」というのが社会的な共通認識であり、マナーです。自分自身の健康を守り、ルールを守ってスマートにVAPEライフをスタートさせるための準備を整えましょう。

title: Terra Incognita
author: Paolo Ottaviani
description:
Terra Incognita explores the untold stories of tech and innovation across emerging markets.
Each episode takes you on a journey through six countries that are rewriting the rules of innovation, far from the usual Silicon Valley spotlight. We tell the stories of the people, the numbers, and the ideas building the future where most people are not looking.
For investors, founders, policy professionals, and anyone curious about what comes next. The companion Substack gives you the full picture, with data and deep dives.

title: Schmidt to Cintas
author: Jene Galvin
description: Welcome to Schmidt to Cintas, conversations about Xavier University's rich basketball success, hosted by two guys who've been there from 1961 to today Jene Galvin and Leo Bradley.

title: The Alvarez Hypothesis
author: Javier Alvarez
description:
What if the greatest experiment you’ll ever run is your own life?


The Alvarez Hypothesis explores one central question:


What happens when ordinary people fully commit to living according to God’s design?


Hosted by Javier, this podcast dives into the intersection of faith, family, leadership, business, discipleship, and everyday life. Each episode examines biblical principles, practical wisdom, and personal experiences that challenge listeners to reject passive living and intentionally build a life that glorifies Christ.


This isn’t about chasing comfort, success, or status.


It’s about forging character, leading your family well, pursuing excellence in your work, building meaningful relationships, and leaving a legacy that points others to Jesus.


Whether you’re a husband, wife, parent, entrepreneur, young professional, or someone simply seeking a deeper purpose, you’ll find conversations that encourage you to think critically, live courageously, and follow Christ faithfully.


Together, we’ll test one hypothesis:


A life surrendered to God is the strongest foundation for a meaningful life, a thriving family, and an enduring legacy.


Faith. Family. Fellowship. Legacy.


Welcome to The Alvarez Hypothesis.

title: The Family Biz Show
author: Michael Palumbos
description:
Join host Michael Palumbos and new guests every episode as they talk about everything from navigating family business transitions, wealth transition, business growth strategies, family conflict, leadership and team development and more. 
Don't forget to share your favorite episodes with others. Tag us with #thefamilybizshow!
If you're a family business or a family business consultant and want to be on the show, share your story and help other family businesses, send us an email to producer@thefamilybizshow.com or visit us at The Family Biz Show | Family Business Podcast With Michael Palumbos (familywealthandlegacy.com) to fill out our web form! 
Securities and investment advisory services offered through Osaic Wealth, Inc. member FINRA/SIPC. Osaic Wealth is separately owned and other entities and/or marketing names, products or services referenced here are independent of Osaic Wealth.

title: Humans are Forever with Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli
author: Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli
description:
Hosted by entrepreneur, innovator, and technology visionary Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli, each episode brings together leading thinkers, scientists, entrepreneurs, educators, and changemakers to examine the evolving relationship between human intelligence and machine intelligence. Through compelling conversations and real-world stories, the show explores how AI is transforming the way we work, create, learn, heal, and connect while asking what will always remain uniquely human.
Join Dr. Ravi Gundlapalli as he explores the future of human intelligence, one conversation at a time, and discovers why, no matter how advanced technology becomes, Humans are Forever.

title: Special Education News and Noteworthy
author: Inclusive Educational Practices
description: Special Education News & Noteworthy is a weekly podcast that breaks down what’s happening in the world of special education—across policy, funding, legal decisions, and community impact. Hosted by Paige Fitzgerald and Kate Magaldi, founders of Inclusive Educational Practices, each episode makes national headlines accessible and actionable for parents, educators, advocates, and anyone who cares about inclusive education.

title: The FAFO Podcast
author: FAFO Podcast
description:
FAFO Podcast Facts. Truth. No BS. This isn’t a safe space—it’s a real space. The FAFO Podcast is where uncomfortable truths get said out loud, weak excuses get exposed, and reality hits exactly the way it’s supposed to. We talk about life, culture, failure, accountability, and the consequences nobody wants to admit exist. No scripts. No filters. No hand-holding. If you’re looking for validation, keep scrolling. If you’re ready for the truth—welcome in. You f*** around… you find out.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/the-fafo-podcast--7183608/support.

title: OSHOISOME
author: sarthak bhagat
description:
Welcome to "OSHOISOME".

Here, we share the wisdom and unique vision of life that Osho brought to the world—not through closed beliefs, but through open eyes, awareness, and understanding.

Join us on a journey of self-discovery, mindfulness, and deeper insights into life. Explore Osho's teachings, timeless truths, and perspectives that encourage us to live consciously, question freely, and see the world with clarity.

Open your eyes. Open your mind. Discover yourself.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/oshoisome--7182815/support.

title: The WELL at College & Dickson Podcast
author: The WELL at College and Dickson
description:
Previously titled: FBCFAYCOLLEGE Podcast
Conversations with Josh and Alison to help students know, trust, love, worship, follow, and obey Jesus more.

title: Wait… What was I gonna say? with Sarah
author: sarahwillmon
description: “wait…what was I gonna say? with sarah” is for anyone whose brain has 37 tabs open and at least one is playing music. Hosted by Sarah: your chaotic, overthinking, oversharing new bestie—this podcast is full of unfiltered rambles, pop culture drama, vivid (and sometimes unhinged) dreams, personal stories, and random tangents that make you forget what you were even talking about. It’s funny, it’s real, it’s messy—grab a snack, get cozy, and let’s spiral.

title: Life in Fife
author: Time Capsule Productions
description:
Life in Fife is an online archive and podcast documenting Fife and all its people from ex-miners to artists and everything in between.
Fife has so much to offer and there are so many great stories in this Kingdom that have never been told, until now. These are businesses, charities, creatives, community groups, and individuals with a story to tell.
Each episode is like a mini documentary creatively produced with the interviews and archive content that have been edited into shorter, enjoyable and easy-to-listen-to episodes.
I’ll record local anniversaries, discussions about former industries and local creatives, musicians and artists. I'll be talking to local businesses, from someone's side hustle to high street names. As well as shining a light on the charities and community groups helping Fife's people in the background. Everyone has stories and unique skills that are valuable to share.
Preventing the loss of local knowledge, highlighting groups that people can get help from or volunteer with and connecting local communities.

title: The Unexplained Archives
author: The Arcane Storyteller
description:
Deep dive into the mysteries that shaped our world.
Government secrets. Military anomalies. The places, events, and moments that official history won't fully explain. From Area 51 to classified 
weapons programs, from UFO sightings to unexplained phenomena — we 
explore what we know, what we don't know, and what the gaps between 
them reveal.
We don't claim to have all the answers. We examine documented evidence. 
We explore declassified documents. We separate fact from speculation.
What we find is often more complex than either the official narrative 
or the conspiracy theories suggest.

title: Yoga with Ramoni Podcast
author: Yoga with Ramoni
description: Yoga is more than what happens on the mat. The Yoga With Ramoni Podcast takes the practice deeper — into relationships, emotions, patterns, and real life. Hosted by yoga innovator and wellness educator Ramoni Overton, each episode connects ancient yoga philosophy to the things you're actually going through.

title: Issues & Challenges In Sterile Processing a Podcast Conversation
author: Jason Marosi
description: A podcast conversation about the problems SPD professionals face every day: Instrument reprocessing, sterilization standards, staffing pressure, leadership challenges, workflow breakdowns, infection prevention, and patient safety.

title: Soul Stories with R.R. Shakti
author: R.R. Shakti, PhD
description: Stories, teachings and reflections from the world of Applied Mythology. R.R. Shakti explores the symbols, archetypes and timeless questions that shape a creative life.

title: Let’s Talk Truths
author: Morgan and Marley
description:
Calling all tweens and teens! Led by BFFs Morgan and Marley, this podcast talks about everything from school to self care. Listen in to hear two girls’ real experiences and thoughts, as well as tips and tricks.
Contact us at LetsTalkTruths863@outlook.com

description: Megan Phelps-Roper was raised inside one of the most infamous religious communities in America. More than a decade after she left, and now a mother herself, she's returning to the Bible — not to reclaim belief, but to ask a harder question: What’s worth keeping?

Through this podcast, you will receive expert insights on leadership exits and transitions, the benefits of interim leadership, and sustainable succession planning in nonprofits. Listen to learn transition strategies for executive director, CEO, and board of directors leadership during resignations, terminations, and unfortunate circumstances such as death.

title: Spine Time
author: Nebraska Spine + Pain Center
description: Your back does a lot for you. It's about time you returned the favor. Spine Time brings physicians, surgeons, physical therapists, and healthcare professionals together for honest conversations about one of the most misunderstood parts of the human body: your back and neck. We bust common myths, answer the questions patients are too nervous to ask in the exam room, and talk about what staying healthy looks like right here in Nebraska.

author: Jean Lordeus
description:
MOVIAA = Men Of Valor In Action Always
The MOVIAA Podcast's goal is to enliven thought-provoking topics and Anecdotes that have fallen by the wayside due to life's busyness. 
The aim is to help someone regain consciousness once more and live. 
MOVIAA will cover a gamut of social issues, Spiritual Revival, Financial Literacy, Self-Worth Awareness, and Uplifting and Promoting young men to their rightful place in this society.

description:
Winning conversations with the men who built their success the right way.
Built to Win, hosted by Seth Irby, sits down with Sigma Phi Epsilon's most accomplished alumni — Fortune 500 CEOs, industry pioneers, and world-renowned physicians — for the real stories and real playbooks behind elite success.
Guests include Bob Eckert (former CEO of Mattel and Kraft Foods), Terry Lundgren (former Chairman & CEO of Macy's), Jeff Henley (Vice Chairman of Oracle), Dr. Jay Harness (internationally recognized breast cancer surgeon), and more.

author: Avery Elise
description: In a world that wants to label you, lift up your banner that tells everyone you have already won the battles you're facing because of Jesus Christ! This podcast gives you big sis advice in hopes to take you from vision to victory. This is "The Banner with Avery Elise."

title: The Severed Thread
author: fromtheashespromotions
description: Avoidant discard isn’t just “they left and didn’t look back.” It’s a whole pattern that trains you to abandon yourself to keep the connection, while they run from their own feelings and call it peace. In this episode we look at both sides of avoidant discard, why it hurts so much, and what it actually takes to start healing instead of chasing.

author: Harry Wareing
description: EHM All Access brings you inside the 'EHM Studio with artist interviews, festival moments, and behind‑the‑scenes stories. Hosted by Harry Wareing, it’s real conversations and real access to the events, artists and music you love. Get it wherever you get your podcasts.

title: Hela Show
author: Hela
description: Hela Show is an unfiltered conversation about the patterns we live, the lessons we almost missed, and the soul work underneath it all — hosted by Hela, nervous system specialist and founder of the HELA Method.

title: Honestly Speaking
author: India Grace
description: Let's get HONEST about life and mental health.Hosted by a psychology student and coming from the perspective of real everyday people, we are talking love, society, consent, religion, personal development and so much more. Come and join the discussion where nothing is off limits. Hosted by India Grace.

title: Medical Class 901 Podcast
author: Betty LaMarr
description:
Medical Class 901 Podcast, hosted by award-winning Memphis Business Journal's healthcare hero Dr. Chinelo Animalu, MD, MPH, FACP, FIDSA,  Professor of Medicine and HIV specialist, addresses critical issues surrounding infectious diseases that impact the younger generation in Memphis, Tennessee, and globally. 


This podcast places a key emphasis on sexually transmitted diseases (STDs) affecting individuals aged 14 to 24. STDs can be devastating and potentially deadly if left untreated. 


Dr. Chinelo Animalu, MD, will provide an in-depth exploration of the various sexually transmitted diseases that are plaguing our communities.

title: Housing Compass: A Navigate Gives Back Podcast
author: Navigate Affordable Housing Partners
description:
Housing Compass is a Navigate Gives Back podcast from Navigate Affordable Housing Partners.
In Season 1, Community Partners, Community Impact, we talk with nonprofit partners working to meet critical needs, support housing stability, and strengthen communities.
Launching August 3, this season features conversations about clean water access, food support, home repair, community revitalization, and housing with dignity.

title: The Other Ireland
author: Nick F
description:
The official community for The Other Ireland podcast.


A place to share and discuss Irish folklore, supernatural encounters, forgotten history, local legends, and the unexplained stories passed down through generations.


Have you heard a strange tale from your town? A family story? A local legend or an unexplained experience?


Share it here and help keep The Other Ireland alive.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: A little bit about us
author: Emilly Villamizar
description: This podcast consists of a small part of our lives and what we want.

title: The Shelf Life of Business
author: Aadhya Guntupalli
description: Join us as we dive into the pages of top business books and unpack real-world examples of marketing, economics, and leadership. Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur or just curious about what makes successful companies work, tune in for fresh perspectives and actionable insights, all through the lens of a high school student.

title: Area of Effect - A TTRPG Show for Parents & Educators
author: The Kids Table Games LLC
description: Did you know that playing D&D can have just as many benefits for families and students as playing sports? Area of Effect is a show where we discuss how tabletop roleplaying games benefit the whole family. As former educators, Ryan and Allison interview experts like TTRPG game designers, educators, psychologists, and more as we give tips to help you bring the adventures to your own table.

title: Streetz Talk podcast
author: Alfie D
description:
Streetz Talk Podcast is a platform for authentic dialogue and community building. We dive deep into the male experience through raw conversations about self-knowledge, life challenges, and aspirations. Join us as we create a space for open expression, share insights, and inspire personal growth.
From heartfelt discussions to electrifying special performances and informative interviews, we bring the energy of the streets to your ears. Don't miss our signature segment, "Sunday's Dinner," where knowledge is always on the menu and thought-provoking convers

title: The Freeway Faith Podcast
author: Freeway Faith
description:
Freeway Faith is a mother-and-son podcast where Gen X and Gen Z perspectives meet at the intersection of faith and everyday life.
Join Heather and Riley as they explore Scripture, ask honest questions, share life experiences, and discuss what it means to follow Jesus in today's world. From Bible study and church history to culture, family, gaming, and unexpected side quests, no topic is off limits.
Pull up a seat, buckle up, and join the conversation. Keep your eyes on the road and your heart on the Word.

title: Who's a good human?
author: Sian Gard
description:
Who's a good human is a podcast all about the relationships humans have with animals.


From cats and dogs, to birds, cows and sheep. Who's a good human is a collection of stories about animals, told by the people that have loved them.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Aggie Effect Podcast
author: Aggie Athletic Foundation
description: The Aggie Effect Podcast is the official podcast of the Aggie Athletic Foundation, celebrating the people, stories, and traditions that strengthen North Carolina A&T State University and Aggie Athletics.  Through engaging conversations with alumni, student-athletes, coaches, university leaders, donors, and community partners, the podcast explores how Aggies are creating excellence, making an impact, and building a lasting legacy.  Each episode highlights the experiences, achievements, and personal journeys that reflect the spirit of Aggie Pride while connecting listeners to the mission of the Aggie Athletic Foundation. From leadership and athletics to philanthropy, service, and the rich history of North Carolina A&T, The Aggie Effect Podcast shares the stories that inspire our community and advance the future of Aggie Athletics.

title: My Texas Ballot
author: Andee Kinzy
description:
What if every voter worked their way through their own ballot before Election Day?
My Texas Ballot is a long-form interview series hosted by Andee Kinzy exploring the people and offices on her Texas ballot. Each episode digs into what the office actually does, why it matters, and the experience each guest brings to public service.
Whether you're preparing to vote or simply want to better understand how local government works, My Texas Ballot goes beyond campaign headlines to help you hear directly from the people on your ballot.
New episodes are released as conversations happen. Follow so you don't miss one.

title: The Women I Worry About.
author: Maggie Lamle
description: The women I worry about is for the women. Everyone depends on the women carrying more than anyone realizes hosted by Maggie Lamle, a former ER and ICU nurse this podcast, explores burnout, resilience, faith, healing, loss, purpose, and what happens when the strong ones finally tell the truth through honest conversations and personal stories you'll find perspective Hope and the reminder that you were never meant to carry everything alone.

title: Late Night Waves
author: Rod Waveeee
description:
Late Night Waves is a podcast for people who feel everything deeply.
If you relate to Rod Wave music, late-night thoughts, heartbreak, and real-life struggles… this is your place.
We talk about pain, growth, loneliness, and turning emotions into strength. Every episode is real, honest, and made for people who don’t always say what they feel — but carry it inside.
This is more than just a podcast.
It’s a feeling.

title: Fireproof Your Money
author: Wayne Firebaugh
description: You've been marketed to your whole life. This show fights back. Hosted by Wayne Firebaugh, CPA and CFP® — a genuine fiduciary who has to put your interests first — Fireproof Your Money busts a costly retirement myth each episode and shows the 50-and-over crowd how to sidestep the preventable mistakes that quietly torch a retirement, from overpaid taxes to high-pressure annuity pitches. Smart, a little mischievous, and always in your corner.

title: Utopia Decision Room
author: Gina Kimiza Tan
description: Utopia Decision Room is a podcast about building a software company in the US — the calls, the pivots, and the decisions founders usually make alone. Every week, I sit down with one founder and unpack one decision that changed how they run their business. Hosted by Utopia Club.

title: The Front Line
author: Pennsylvania Department of Military and Veterans Affairs
description:
Join us on the front line for The Front Line Podcast
Whether you are a veteran, service member, military family member, or a dedicated advocate, this podcast speaks directly to your experience. We sit down with influential leaders across the commonwealth who are on the front lines fighting to improve the lives of those who served. So, march into the fray with us as we stand shoulder-to-shoulder, navigating the high-speed operational tempo of military and veterans affairs.

title: THE BEFORE AND BEYOND PODCAST.
author: Oluwanifesimi Akilapa
description:
Welcome to the Before and Beyond Podcast.
We are here to talk real stories- from who we were to who we are becoming. 
This is the space where the past meets with purpose. 
Grab a seat, you are about to get transformed.
Subscribe now to join the family.
You can send an email to thebeforeandbeyondpodcast@gmail.com for enquiries.
For paid partnerships, you can send an email to thebeforeandbeyondpodcast@gmail.com
If you will like to give your life to Christ, you can send an email to thebeforeandbeyondpodcast@gmail.com for guidance. 
Thank you and welcome once again.

title: The Short List
author: HustleStudios Podcast Network
description: Ultra-brief daily news with personality. Not neutral. Takes a stance. Five minutes of what to know and what to think.

title: Daily Bread
author: Emmanuel John
description:
Daily Bread is a short daily Bible podcast created to bring peace, hope, and encouragement through scripture, reflection, and prayer.
Each episode features calming Bible readings, thoughtful devotional messages, and simple prayers designed to strengthen your faith and help you stay connected to God throughout the day.
Whether you are starting your morning, ending your evening, or searching for peace in difficult moments, Daily Bread is here to help you slow down, reflect, and rest in God’s presence.
New episodes released daily.

title: Own Your Revival
author: Andy Arbie
description: Welcome to the Own Your Revival podcast hosted by Andy Arbie, Catholic Life Coach, for faithful women who know the pathway to more peace, joy and purpose is found when we take radical responsibility for ourselves.   Because when we do, we step into the fullness of who we were made to be by a God who loves us a lot.   Join us every week as we keep it real and have fun sharing trials, tips and testimony to grow from where we are to where God is calling us to be.

title: Crazy Brit Asian
author: Chris Mak
description: What's up ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the Crazy Brit Asian Podcast where we document the nuances of the British East Asian experience. That means talking about a wide range of topics around all things Asian, not just in the UK but beyond! It's an ever evolving area of conversation so if you can think about it, we're probably going to talk about it. Each episode we delve into a conversational cultural exploration with a guest because who better to talk about something than someone who's lived it? Real people, real talk, real life experiences IG: https://www.instagram.com/crazybritasian

title: The Brisbane Bullets Podcast
author: Ollie Godden
description: Hosted by NBL Royalty and Bullets captain Mitch Norton, The Brisbane Bullets Podcast goes under the bonnet to bring you closer to the action on the court and all the stories off it.

title: Sad satan
author: Nicolás Dagostino
description: paranormal

title: Dannie B Speaks...The Podcast
author: Dannie B Speaks
description: This podcast is an extension of my former website danniebspeaks.com where any and everything is discussed.

title: Fractured But Fearless
author: Dr. S. Irfan Ali
description:
Fractured but Fearless is a podcast about the journey most leaders never talk about.
Dr. Irfan Ali came to America with only $1,700. He cleaned carpets at night while attending medical school during the day. 
Through discipline, risk, and resilience, he built one of the largest privately held physician groups in West Florida.
On this podcast, he sits down with founders, physicians, entrepreneurs, artists, and executives who are willing to speak honestly about:
    • The failures that shaped them
    • ﻿﻿The losses that forced growth
    • ﻿﻿The resilience required when no one is watching
    • ﻿﻿The risks no one sees
    • ﻿﻿The turning points that changed everything

title: Building One Piece at a Time
author: Patriot Talk 920 AM
description:
Building One Piece at a Time is an honest journey of faith, family, and entrepreneurship.

I'm Matt Velasquez—a husband, father, Christian, and owner of Patriot Talk 920 AM in Houston. After losing both of my parents, I was left with the responsibility of carrying on our family's 40-year radio legacy. Instead of staying with what was familiar, I took a leap of faith and completely reinvented our family business.


This podcast is my honest look at what happens after you take that leap—the wins, the losses, the mistakes, the financial struggles, the relationships, and the lessons I'm learning along the way.

I don't have all the answers, and this isn't a story being told from the finish line. I'm still in the middle of it, trying to trust God's plan, honor my family's legacy, and build something that can make a lasting difference in my hometown of Houston.


Because the things that matter most aren't built overnight. They're built one piece at a time.

author: UNM IM Chiefs
description: Weekly clinical pearls for the busy IM resident. Recorded by the University of New Mexico Internal Medicine Chief Residents.

title: The Womben Wellness Podcast
author: Usha Anandi

The Womben Wellness Podcast with Usha Anandi is a podcast for women breaking cycles and reclaiming their power through womb healing, cyclical living, and feminine wisdom. Hosted by holistic nutritionist, childbirth educator, full-spectrum doula, folk herbalist, and yoga teacher trainer Usha Anandi, each episode blends science and the sacred to help you live in rhythm with your body and bring the healing your lineage has been waiting for.

Each episode unpacks the science of how your body regulates itself, why presence matters, and how to intentionally shift your state. Designed for coaches, facilitators, healers, and anyone building a personal brand through embodiment — this is where nervous system literacy meets practical application.

description: Behind the Wall Podcast is Drillcut’s straight-talking podcast hosted by Paul Higginson, bringing industry stories, pressure points and real conversations into one place. From construction and plumbing challenges to business growth, pricing pressure and the people behind the work, each episode goes beyond the surface to explore what is really happening across the trade.

title: Journey of Gratitude
author: Ruben Navarrete, Tri Robinson
description: Hosted by Ruben Navarrete and author/pastor Tri Robinson, The Journey of Gratitude is a deep-dive conversation centered around Tri’s book, A Journey of Gratitude: Traversing Four Seasons of Life. Together, Ruben and Tri go beyond the pages of the book to explore the profound, sometimes messy, and always miraculous ways that God directs our paths. Part memoir and part spiritual retrospective, this podcast is a guide for anyone looking back at where they've been to better understand where God is leading them today.

title: reCourage
author: Gateway Seminary
description:
The reCourage Podcast is a companion to the B&H book Your Finest Hour: Faithful Ministry during Discouraging Times by Gateway Seminary faculty.

Pastoral ministry can be demanding, stressful and discouraging. Pastors are constantly giving but not always pausing to refuel. At times they feel overwhelmed, isolated, and defeated. 

"Recouraged" was a word coined by playwright John Heywood in the mid 1500’s. Though it does not enjoy inclusion in modern dictionaries, it does carry the force of what we present in Your Finest Hour; You matter to God.

title: Spotlight Society
author: Deb Allen
description:
Spotlight Society is a podcast that celebrates the radiance, resilience, and realness of women redefining life on their own terms. Hosted by Deb Allen, each episode puts a powerful woman in the spotlight, diving into beauty, wellness, bounce-back moments, and life lessons with grace and authenticity. Whether you’re entering a new chapter, chasing a dream, or reclaiming your confidence, this is your space to shine.
Tune in for raw conversations, radiant stories, and tips that inspire you to glow from the inside out.

title: The Adventurer's

description: Join Kale, Isaac, and Jake as they embark on a world tour of the wonders of the world and their myths and secrets. We talk about mountains, animals, National Parks, and several others. Come back regularly for new episodes.

title: Health Notes with Dr. Bob
author: Health Notes with Dr. Bob
description: Health Notes with Dr. Bob is a health and wellness podcast hosted by Dr. Bob Driver, a board-certified physician and founder of Spartan Medical Associates. The show answers real patient and viewer questions across a wide range of health topics, turning them into clear, actionable guidance on nutrition, exercise, sleep, and mental well-being. The goal: help everyday listeners become smarter, more informed patients who can make better decisions about their own health.

title: The Author Path with GracePoint Publishing
author: GracePoint
description:
GracePoint: The Author Path

Welcome to GracePoint: The Author Path, the podcast for writers, authors, coaches, experts, and visionary voices who are ready to bring their message into the world through books.

Hosted by Michelle Vandepas, co-founder of GracePoint Publishing, this show explores the real path of authorship: writing, publishing, marketing, visibility, distribution, mindset, and the business of becoming a published author.

Each episode offers practical guidance, behind-the-scenes publishing insight, author conversations, and grounded encouragement for anyone who feels called to write, publish, and share their work.

Whether you are writing your first book, relaunching an existing one, building your author platform, or wondering what it really takes to publish well, The Author Path will help you move forward with more clarity, confidence, and direction.

GracePoint Publishing helps authors create books with meaning, quality, and purpose.

title: Lessons from the Sensei, Law, Business and Life
author: lessonsfromthesensei
description: Lessons from the Sensei, Law, Business and Life is a podcast dedicated to exploring the intersection of legal insights, business acumen, and personal growth. Hosted by Mike Leon, the show features conversations with experienced attorneys and professionals who share their knowledge and stories. Listeners gain practical advice, ethical considerations, and strategic approaches to navigating complex legal and business landscapes. Whether you're a legal professional, business owner, or simply curious about the law, this podcast provides valuable lessons and inspiration.

title: Friends with Benefits
author: Priyanka Nair and Yulea Roopai
description:
A podcast created by two real-life best friends who are unafraid to talk about everything you’re already thinking but haven’t said out loud yet. From voice notes that became therapy to couch chats that turned into confessions, this show was born from friendship and fire - reflective, and sometimes ridiculous. We’ll drop into your ears with stories about love, work, adulting, glow-ups, heartbreak, personal growth, and all the things that happen between the big moments.
“We’re not experts. We’re just real friends figuring it out with you.”

title: Crimes Of Fashion
author: Ilyana “Red” Pastranos
description:
Welcome to Crimes of Fashion 
Crimes of Fashion is a fashion commentary podcast exploring the psychology, culture, scandals, obsessions, and behaviors that shape the fashion industry.
Hosted by Ilyana “Red” Pastranos, each episode investigates the emotional forces behind luxury, status, influence, identity, consumer behavior, and the stories people tell through what they wear.
Through cultural commentary, marketing analysis, fashion history, and industry critique, Crimes of Fashion examines the relationship between fashion and human behavior—from aspiration and belonging to fraud, counterfeits, influence, and the darker side of the industry.
Because fashion is never really just about clothes.
It’s about people.
Stay observant.

A deep dive into the journeys and lives of musicians and music professionals.

author: Chronicles of Japan
description: Step back in time and explore the rich tapestry of Japanese history. "Chronicles of Japan" brings you captivating stories of legendary samurai, ancient emperors, powerful shoguns, and the cultural milestones that shaped the Land of the Rising Sun. From the ancient Jomon period to the modern Meiji Restoration, we delve deep into the battles, philosophies, and daily lives of historical figures. Join us on an educational journey to uncover the truths, myths, and legends of Japan’s fascinating past. Subscribe now to never miss an episode of history coming alive.

title: The Near Death Files
author: Wes Krueger
description: Near Death Files explores the world's most fascinating near-death experiences. Each episode dives into real stories of people who were declared clinically dead or came close to death and returned with extraordinary accounts of tunnels of light, out-of-body experiences, heavenly visions, unexplained encounters, and life-changing revelations. Whether viewed through science, medicine, spirituality, or mystery, these stories examine one of humanity's oldest questions: What happens when we die?

description: The Corporate Cup is a satirical podcast about the absurdity of modern office life, from performance reviews that mean nothing to buzzwords that mean even less. Hosted by Lane Synergy, a corporate veteran who's sat through one too many 'quick syncs,' the show pokes fun at the culture we all secretly complain about. New episodes drop regularly. Pour your coffee and get ready to nod along uncomfortably.

title: The Squall Line Podcast
author: Rick Fiske
description:
On this podcast, we'll talk about weather through the eyes of a 23-year Air Force veteran, share stories of warriors who served our nation, honor the veterans I've met—and those I've had the privilege to lay to rest—and explore the mighty works of God's grace in my life and in the lives of others over the past 50 years.

Whether you're fascinated by storms, inspired by service, or encouraged by stories of faith and redemption, there's something here for you.

So, settle in, and let's get started. Welcome to the Squall Line Podcast.

author: St. Louis Catholic Church
description: Three Priests. One Gospel. A weekly conversation on the Sunday Gospel.

title: Lighthouse Brotherhood
author: Matt Balducci and Ruslan Ilnitsky
description: For the man people count on, who has no one to count on. You built the business. You provide. From the outside, you're winning. But the house gets quiet, and you wonder if you're the husband, father, and leader you meant to be, or just the guy holding it together alone. Matt Balducci and Ruslan Ilnitsky work one real problem high-achieving men carry in silence each episode, through the Five Identities: Husband, Father, Leader, Anchor, and Athlete. No hype. No warrior act. Real stories, real frameworks, and the standard you set when you stop drifting. If this is you, you're not the only one.

title: From Message to Published with GracePoint Publishing hosted by Michelle Vandepas, publisher.
author: Michelle Vandepas
description: From Message to Published is the insider's guide to getting your book into the world. Publisher Michelle Vandepas — founder of women-owned GracePoint Publishing — interviews authors and industry experts, covering the full publishing journey from first draft to bookstore shelf. Whether you're exploring self-publishing, hybrid publishing, or traditional publishing, Michelle brings real conversations, insider strategies, and deep expertise in book distribution to help you go from message to published.

title: Mindful, Kindful Living
author: Connie Warden
description:
Life doesn’t always go as planned. We get stressed. We react. We miss the exit. And sometimes… we forget to be kind to ourselves along the way.


Welcome to Mindful, Kindful Living — a podcast for people who want to slow down, laugh at their humanity, and live with more mindfulness and compassion.

I’m Connie Warden, mindfulness teacher and author of Speeding to Compassion, and each week I sit down with thoughtful guests to explore real-life stories — the messy moments, the turning points, and the small shifts that help us move from reaction to reflection.


This podcast is for you if you:

  • feel the pressure to be perfect
  • know that your health is your wealth.
  • want to respond to life with more calm and clarity
  • are curious about mindfulness in everyday situations
  • or simply want to feel a little more at ease being human


We talk about things like emotional intelligence, stress, relationships, resilience, and self-compassion — all through the lens of real stories and honest conversation, with just enough humor to keep things light.

New episodes drop weekly on Wednesdays and each one is an invitation to take a breath, reset, and gently steer back toward what matters.


If that sounds like something you need, hit follow, and come join us in Mindful, Kindful Living.

title: Kids Story Time | Magical Flo Adventures for Kids Ages 3-9
author: Tracey Tripp
description:
Magical bedtime and story-time adventures for kids ages 3-9! Join Magical Flo and her cat as they explore a world of toadstool houses, talking trees, mischievous pixies, and everyday magic.


Each episode is a fun, imaginative story perfect for story time, car rides, or winding down before bed — full of gentle adventure, counting, and a sprinkle of wonder.


These are the audio versions of the Magical Flo animated series! Watch the full videos, songs, and more on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@magicalflokids


New episodes released regularly — follow so you never miss an adventure!


Perfect for kids who love: bedtime stories, story time, children's stories, kids adventure stories, and magical tales for preschoolers and young children.

Category: File this under "Kids & Family" — this is the standard category Spotify and Apple Podcasts use, and it's what determines whether you show up in kids-specific browsing/charts, so it's worth getting right rather than defaulting to something broader like "Stories" or "Arts."

Episode title formula — worth using this consistently, since individual episode titles are searched/indexed too, not just the show title:



Ep 1: Flo and the Dragon's Egg | Magical Flo Kids Story

Cover art specs (this trips a lot of people up): design at 3000×3000px, square, JPEG format, kept under 512KB file size — Apple Podcasts enforces this strictly even though you're publishing via Spotify, since most distributors push to both platforms from one asset. Bold, simple text and a clear character (Flo's face works well) reads better at the tiny thumbnail sizes podcast art actually gets seen at.

One thing worth deciding now: since your show name has a 100-character limit on Spotify, and you'll want each episode title to also carry good keywords, it's worth keeping the show title itself fairly lean (like above) and letting each episode's individual title do more of the specific-keyword work — that way you're not trying to cram everything into one title field.

well a bit of a mix


Reconciled contextual ambiguity and revised content description accordingly

These are the audio versions of the Magical Flo animated series! Watch the full videos, songs, and more on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@magicalflokids


New episodes released regularly — follow so you never miss an adventure!

title: The Gifted PLaCe
author: Sonu Aziz
description:
About The Gifted PLaCe


Connecting Research, Educators, and Practice to Advance Understanding of Neurodiverse Learners


The Gifted PLaCe was created to bring together researchers, educators, and practitioners to explore current research, emerging ideas, and meaningful practices that support neurodiverse learners. Drawing on her experience as an educator, researcher, and scholar-practitioner, Dr. Aziz brings together conversations with leaders in the field to explore topics related to gifted and advanced learners, twice- and thrice-exceptional students, curriculum and instruction, educator development, and the evolving landscape of education. The Gifted PLaCe aims to bridge research and practice by creating opportunities for educators to engage with new perspectives, reflect on their work, and consider approaches that support the academic, social, and emotional development of learners with diverse strengths, abilities, and ways of experiencing the world.


About Dr. Sonu Aziz


Dr. Sonu Aziz is an educator, researcher, and scholar-practitioner whose work bridges gifted education, curriculum development, educator preparation, and educational research.
As Head of Academic Affairs at Long Island School for the Gifted, Dr. Aziz supports curriculum development, faculty learning, and instructional practices across grades K–8. Her work focuses on creating responsive learning environments that challenge and support learners while helping educators develop the knowledge, skills, and perspectives needed to meet the needs of diverse learners.


Dr. Aziz holds a Certificate in Advanced Gifted Education, and her scholarly interests center on gifted learners, including twice- and thrice-exceptional students, and the ways educational systems can better recognize and develop diverse expressions of ability. Through her doctoral research in Leadership & Innovation at New York University, which examined teacher perceptions of twice-exceptional students and received the Distinguished Problem of Practice Award, Dr. Aziz explores how research can inform practices that strengthen identification, instruction, and support for neurodiverse learners. Her engagement with the field extends beyond her research and school-based work through her service on the Public Policy and Advocacy Committee for the National Association for Gifted Children and her contributions to GiftedNYS.


Her work is grounded in a commitment to connecting research with practice and helping educators create learning environments where students feel challenged, supported, and valued. In recognition of her contributions to gifted education, Dr. Aziz received the Dr. Rosemary Callard-Szulgit Student Award for Gifted Education in 2026.


Through The Gifted PLaCe, Dr. Aziz continues to create opportunities for educators and researchers to engage in thoughtful conversations that deepen understanding of neurodiverse learners and translate research into meaningful educational practice. Her work reflects a commitment to helping educators recognize student potential, develop responsive approaches, and create learning environments where students’ strengths and pathways for growth are understood and supported.

title: ACLED Audio
author: ACLED (Armed Conflict Location and Event Data)
description:
Brought to you by ACLED, the world's leading source of real-time data and analysis on political violence and protest, ACLED Audio brings expert insight directly to your audio feed.

title: Dopamine + Doubt: Untangling ADHD and OCD

title: Tell Me Something Messy with Brandon Kyle Goodman
author: Brandon Kyle Goodman
description: Tell Me Something Messy is your unfiltered companion for relationships, adulting, and defeating shame! Each week, writer and comedian Brandon Kyle Goodman invites listeners (aka Messy Mob) to leave perfectionism at the door and wade into the wonderfully unhinged world of dating, personal growth, and getting comfortable with disappointing your mom! (Seriously, it’s okay!) With candid guests and plenty of messy giggles, BKG spills the kind of piping hot tea that compassionately emboldens listeners to say the quiet things out loud and embrace their messiest truths! Don’t be shy baby, tell us something messy! SomethingMessy.com WATCH HERE!

author: Aaron Wright
description: Get ready to align your financial strategies with your deeply held values! Aligning your money with your beliefs, Aaron Wright empowers you with a plan you can believe in.

title: Ghosts: Spoken Essays
author: Ghosts Movement: Pedro Malha
description: Ghosts: Spoken Essays presents original essays from the Ghosts Movement, written and spoken by Pedro Malha, philosopher and founder of the movement. Each episode is a complete essay, read aloud as it was written, without commentary or discussion. The Ghosts Movement is a quiet philosophy of presence, memory, and recognition. These essays move through everyday life, relationship, time, absence, and the unseen structures that shape a life, offered as a space to listen and return.

title: Talking Headless with Shashi Bellamkonda - Playaz Productions Network
author: Playaz Productions Network
description:
Talking Headless with Shashi Bellamkonda is a premier business technology broadcast featured on the Playaz Productions Network (PPN) YouTube channel.

Originally created and hosted by industry veteran Paul Greenberg, the show entered an exciting new chapter in mid-2025 when marketing and technology leader Shashi Bellamkonda took over as the host.

Show Overview & Key Themes
The Analyst Relations Ecosystem: The show specializes in demystifying the relationship between technology vendors, Analyst Relations (AR) professionals, and industry analysts. It explores how these groups collaborate to shape industry narratives and drive market visibility.

Emerging Tech & AI: Episodes frequently explore cutting-edge topics, such as how Artificial Intelligence (AI) and AI assistants are transforming the analyst ecosystem and vendor-analyst communications.

Expert Guests: Shashi regularly interviews top-tier industry players, including global heads of AR from major firms (such as Databricks and Wiz), principal research analysts, and tech founders.

Real-World Playbooks: Moving past mere hypothetical discussions, the episodes offer actionable advice, real-world success stories, and deep dives into enterprise technology trends.

Typically streamed live on LinkedIn, YouTube, and other video platforms, the show is highly regarded in the B2B tech community for bridging the gap between industry insiders and technology buyers.

title: Behind the Credits with Lisa Rodríguez
author: Lisa Rodríguez
description: *Behind the Credits with Lisa Rodríguez* pulls back the curtain on the entertainment industry from every angle — the business, the craft, and the deeply human experience of a life in this industry. From producers and directors to casting directors, crew, and everyone in between, Lisa brings you candid conversations about how projects actually get made — and how you can make them yourself. With three generations of entertainment industry experience behind her, Lisa doesn't just talk about the business — she is the business. And she's not afraid to get real. Because behind every credit is a story, and those stories deserve to be told.

title: The Spatial Computing Podcast

description: Welcome to The Spatial Computing Podcast, where we explore XR, AI, wearables, robotics and the technologies shaping the next generation of human-computer interaction.

description: In this podcast, we introduce ourselves as students of English Course 3 at Universidad Tecnológica de Pereira. Although we study different careers, we share the same passion for learning and achieving our dreams.

title: The Grumpy Social Workers
author: Soyeb Aswat and Siobhan Maclean
description:
The Grumpy Social Workers is a podcast hosted by Soyeb Aswat and Siobhan Maclean, bringing honest, thoughtful, and occasionally grumpy conversations about the realities of the social work sector. 
The podcast explores the challenges, pressures, practice issues, policy changes, and professional conversations shaping social work today, offering insight, reflection, humour, and straight-talking discussion for social workers and those across the profession.

title: The Green Beret Way Podcast
author: Robert Schaefer
description:
I’m Robert W. Schaefer—retired Green Beret, senior diplomat, and leadership strategist. With 40+ years advising heads of state, top military leaders, and Fortune 500 execs. I now bring Special Forces leadership tactics to business, teams, and life.


Learn how to think fast, lead strong, and execute like an elite operator.


Follow for powerful strategies from my upcoming book, The Green Beret Way: Leading Elite Teams Under Adverse Conditions.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Practical AI Podcast
author: Taylor
description:
The Practical AI Podcast with Hashi Sivananthan, Founder and Principal at Digiform


AI doesn't have to be confusing — and it definitely doesn't have to be hype.


Every week, AI adoption and governance expert Hashi breaks down what's actually happening in AI and what it means for your business. No jargon. No buzzwords. Just simple, straightforward guidance for non-technical business leaders who want to drive real results with AI — without breaking what already works.

From customer experience and personalization to governance, guardrails, and knowing when not to use AI, each episode gives you practical takeaways you can bring to your next leadership meeting.

Because good AI starts with good decisions.


New episodes weekly. Follow Hashi on Instagram @aimehashi.

title: Would You Laugh At This
author: Holly and Jess
description: Welcome to our podcast where we talk about everything from pop culture to personal problems, We hope you laugh with us and learn a little something along the way.

title: School Fees with James Bradnick
author: Urban Poetry
description:
Every lesson in business has a price. Some people pay it in money, some in time, some in sleep. In South Africa we call that price school fees — and someone has already paid yours.
Each episode, James Bradnick sits down with founders, executives and specialists who've paid expensive lessons, and gets them on record: what happened, what it actually cost, and the rule they now live by. Real stories, real numbers, no fluff.
One rule on this show: we put numbers on things.
New episodes monthly from Urban Poetry. Class is in session — don't pay fees someone's already paid.

title: Dive With Me
author: Dive With Me
description:
Have you been through the worst time in your life and made it through to the other side?
Join me on a new podcast that explores real stories of struggle, pain, resilience, and healing. This is a safe, completely anonymous space. No names. No identifying details. Just raw, honest conversations that reminds others they’re not alone.

title: PROVIDENCE ST
author: Tatiana Smolina & Arjun Rull
description:
We're Tatiana & Arjun, and we just launched your new favourite audio podcast!
🍷🥃 It's busy, it's authentic and it's just.. life!
We started this podcast out of curiosity, a lot of mutual respect, and a love for deep, unfiltered conversations. For us, no topic is too small, and no perspective is off-limits.
Consider this your official invite into the beginnings of our judgment-free zone. We’re here to question the norm, explore everyday topics through a fresh lens, and focus on what brings us all together rather than what drives us apart. We’re learning as we go, and we want you to join us!

title: Witness to Success
author: Troy Korsgaden
description: Troy Korsgaden shares his exclusive conversations with the most successful players in the insurance industry and beyond. Learn from the success stories of field leaders, industry icons and the upcoming pioneers. Troy has witnessed some of the best in action and now brings you exclusive access to their stories.

title: Queen B: Grace, Grit and Reinvention
author: Steve Koch
description: Success is never a straight line.  Before the luxury real estate, before the billion-dollar business, before the recognition, Valerie Fitzgerald was a single mother with no safety net, no business experience, and no roadmap—just a determination to create a better future.  Queen B: Grace, Grit and Reinvention is a podcast about rebuilding your life, embracing change, and discovering what's possible when you refuse to quit on yourself.  Each week, Valerie shares the lessons she's learned through decades of building one of Los Angeles' most successful luxury real estate businesses, alongside honest conversations with entrepreneurs, business leaders, authors, and inspiring people who have navigated setbacks, reinvented themselves, and created extraordinary lives.  Together, they'll explore topics like:  Building confidence before you feel ready Reinventing yourself after life's biggest challenges Leadership, resilience, and personal growth Wealth, purpose, and creating opportunities Real estate as a pathway to freedom and long-term security The life lessons success can never teach on its own  Whether you're starting over, chasing a dream, navigating change, or simply looking for the courage to take the next step, this podcast is here to remind you that your story isn't defined by where you begin—it's shaped by the decisions you make along the way.  Because confidence isn't built before action.  Confidence is built through action.

title: Are You There Yet?
author: By Carmen & Anna
description:
Are You There Yet? is the conversation you have with your best friend when the coffee is gone, the masks are off, and nobody is pretending to have it all figured out.
Join us as we explore the questions, challenges, laughs, and lessons that come with being human. From relationships and family to healing, purpose, spirituality, and those unexpected detours life throws our way — we’re figuring it out one conversation at a time.
Because the truth is… none of us really knows exactly where we’re going.
But maybe the journey is the point.
So… are you there yet?

title: Socket 742: En
author: Marco Jimenez
description:
Do you feel like you arrived too late to artificial intelligence? You didn’t.
Socket 742 explores creativity, technology, and the human layer behind every project. Using the author’s real experiences, notes and past work, AI tools transform that material into dynamic conversations about learning, creating, and reviving ideas once left behind.
The raw material is human. The voice is digital. The purpose is not to replace human creativity, but to help us reconnect with what we have always wanted to build.
This is Socket 742. Plug into the conversation. Where human stories meet digital voices.

title: Salty Saints
author: Hayden Holm
description: Inspiring Latter-day Saint content, without the spirit voice or green jell-o, to strengthen your faith, family, and community. Join us as we navigate staying faithful in spite of the world, the culture, and social media. Tune in for debunkings, discussions on gospel topics, missionary stories, cultural callouts, and navigating questions faithfully.

title: Bowl of Data - The Podcast
author: Marco Parrillo
description:
Bowl of Data is a short weekly briefing on the tech that matters: AI, cybersecurity, blockchain, quantum computing, and software engineering. Every week, host Marco cuts through the noise to cover the stories, research, and model releases actually worth your time: no hype, no filler, just a direct, occasionally witty read for engineers, founders, and tech-savvy investors who don't have time for fifty newsletters. New episodes every week.
Website: https://www.bowlofdata.net/

title: Bloom to Become
author: Arya Hassan
description:
Bloom to Become is your go-to podcast for all things self-growth, wellness, and girl talk. Hosted by Arya Hassan, this podcast is like having comforting conversations with your older sister, or being on FaceTime with your best friend! 
Arya covers everything from building confidence and staying consistent, to navigating life experiences, habits, and finally becoming the person you want to be.
Come as you are, and grow at your own pace loves 🌺

title: English Conversation Hub
author: Lao
description:
Welcome to English Conversation Hub—your trusted place to learn real, natural, and confident English through engaging conversations and practical lessons.

Whether you're a beginner or an advanced learner, each episode is designed to improve your listening, speaking, vocabulary, pronunciation, and everyday communication skills. Learn authentic English used by native speakers in daily life, travel, work, and social situations.

Our mission is to help you speak English with confidence through easy-to-understand explanations, real-life dialogues, useful expressions, and interactive practice.

title: For Your Ears Only
author: Kay Camp
description:
Welcome to my personal audio diary!

On this pod, I talk about feelings, past relationships, being a parent, and just life in general!

title: The Graveyard Slot
author: The Graveyard Slot
description:
It's 3 a.m. and the station should be dead air. Instead, someone is reading you a story.

The Graveyard Slot is a horror fiction anthology broadcast from the loneliest shift on the dial. Each episode, your host takes the overnight desk and reads one complete tale... no cliffhangers, no filler. Original scary stories about small towns, empty highways, night shifts, trail cameras that see too much, and the quiet ways ordinary lives come apart.

Slow-burn dread over jump scares. Every story finishes before the sun comes up.

New episodes twice a week. Best heard alone, after midnight, with headphones. All stories are original works of fiction.

title: The Tech Society
author: The Tech Society
description: This is the Tech Society podcast hosted by Caden and co-host Alexis

title: The Reading Success Podcast
author: SOR Tutoring
description: The Reading Success Podcast by SOR Tutoring, featuring insights from https://www.sor-tutoring.com, helps K-8 students overcome reading and writing challenges with expert online tutoring guidance, literacy tips, and dyslexia support.

title: American Regret
author: American Regret
description:
Smart people don't ruin their lives by accident. They do it by being convincing — and the person they convince is themselves.

American Regret is a forensic examination of how capable, self-aware people talk themselves into the choices that end their lives as they knew them — and how the rest of us are doing the exact same thing right now, just with smaller stakes and no federal agents involved yet.

Every episode takes one person who had everything to lose and lost it: the cancer researcher who trusted the wrong person, the dealer who cooperated with the DEA for six years and called it bad luck, the Border Patrol agent who refused to cooperate and did the time. We don't tell their stories for the shock. We dissect them for the pattern — the rationalization, the language people use on themselves, the four seconds of hearing yourself clearly that separates the people who stayed free from the ones who didn't.

This isn't true crime and it isn't self-help. There's no healing arc, no five steps, no redemption on a schedule. Just the unflinching architecture of self-deception, examined by someone who built her own and did her time taking it apart.

Your host is Deana Martin — the Fairy GodFelon. She's not a doctor or a coach. She's the smartest person who was ever wrong about everything, finally telling the truth. New episodes every two weeks.

Listen close. You're going to recognize someone. The problem is it's going to be you.

title: Hello, Little Dreamer- Kids Sleep Stories
author: Melissa and Clair Brown
description:
Hello, Little Dreamer is designed to help kids wind down, regulate their big feelings, and drift to sleep feeling safe, worthy and loved.
Each episode gently weaves:
• Emotional intelligence and resilience skills
• Age-appropriate coping tools
• Kind inner dialogue
• Family connection themes
• Light, comforting fantasy elements
• Slow pacing for real nervous system calm
These aren’t fast, flashy stories. They are intentional, soothing bedtime journeys that help children process their day, understand their feelings, and build confidence from the inside out.
Parents can expect stories that:
• Normalize big emotions like frustration, jealousy, sadness, and worry
• Model healthy communication and repair
• Teach regulation tools like grounding, breathwork, body awareness, and perspective-taking
• Reinforce belonging, teamwork, and unconditional love
• Encourage independence and self-trust
• Close gently, without abrupt endings or stimulation spikes
At the heart of every episode is one core message:
You are safe. You are loved. You are learning.
Our Values
We believe:
• Children deserve emotional education woven into everyday life.
• Parents deserve support, not shame.
• Imagination is a powerful tool for resilience.
• Bedtime can be connection, not conflict.
And most importantly:
Hello, Little Dreamer will always remain ad-free.
No interruptions.
No sponsored messages.
No products inserted into your child’s wind-down routine.
Your child’s nervous system is not for sale.
This podcast exists to create a calm, consistent bedtime ritual you can trust.
So snuggle in.
Dim the lights.
Take a slow breath.
And let’s begin…
“Hello, Little Dreamer…”

title: Leaning Into The FIRE: couples therapy conversations
author: Ed Peterson
description:
LEANING INTO THE FIRE: couples therapy conversation is a podcast about the real work of couples therapy—what actually happens in the room when relationships are struggling and people are trying to change. Hosted by Ed Peterson, LCSW and EFT Supervisor, the show explores the emotional patterns that keep couples stuck and the moments that help them find each other again. Through thoughtful conversations about attachment, conflict, vulnerability, and repair, the podcast offers a window into how meaningful change actually happens in relationships.

Each episode reflects on the courage it takes to face hard conversations rather than avoid them. Drawing from Emotionally Focused Therapy, attachment science, and years of clinical experience, the podcast explores how couples move from blame and distance toward understanding, safety, and deeper connection. Episodes may include reflections on real therapy dynamics, discussions with other therapists, and practical insights that help people understand the emotional cycles that shape their relationships.

LEANING INTO THE FIRE: couples therapy conversation: is for therapists, couples, and anyone curious about the deeper emotional forces that drive love, conflict, and reconciliation. Rather than offering quick tips or surface-level advice, the podcast focuses on the deeper work of emotional healing—helping people understand their own fears, longings, and attachment needs so they can build stronger, more secure relationships. 🔥

title: SuperPod Saga
author: Aaron Klaassen
description: A chill podcast that discusses every video game topic in existence, one episode at a time.

title: Texas Trash Titans
author: Texas Trash Titans
description: At Texas Trash Titans, our approach to junk removal in Houston, TX is centered on efficiency, reliability, and customer care. We know that every cleanup project is unique, so we take the time to understand your needs and provide tailored solutions that fit your schedule and budget.

title: TRUCK
author: Alex Vespestad
description:
There's something about being in an old car. Is it loud? Yes. Does the AC work? Absolutely not. Are the seatbelts load-bearing? Debatable. But outside the window everything shifts to another place and time.


Podcast journalist Alex Vespestad picks up musicians in vintage rides and drives the California coast. They talk career highs, late nights, the stories that've never made it to an interview. And when the conversation reaches an end, they get in the back for a serenade under the open sky.


It's a feeling you've known your whole life...you've just never heard it like this.

title: The DubLine Podcast
author: The DubLine Podcast
description: Welcome to The DubLine Podcast, where sports, competition, and storytelling come together. Hosted by Kam Kaiser and Cole Schweda, the show features intense conversations, mini-games, and unique insights into the sporting world. From athlete stories and hot takes to behind-the-scenes experiences and friendly competition, each episode brings a fresh perspective on the games, people, and moments that make sports so captivating.
